result.stdout:
Description:
  Velopack CLI 0.0.1350-l10n.14-g48cbddf546 (prerelease), for distributing      
  applications.                                                                 

Usage:
  vpk [command] [options]  

Options:
  -h, -H, --help, --vhelp  Show help (-h) or extended help (-H).                
  -x, --legacyConsole      Disable console colors and interactive               
                           components.                                          
  -y, --yes                'yes' by instead of 'no' in non-interactive          
                           prompts.                                             
  --verbose                Print diagnostic messages.                           
  --skip-updates           Skip update checks                                   

Commands:
  pack      Create a Linux .AppImage bundle from application files.         
  download  Download's the latest release from a remote update source.      
  upload    Upload local package(s) to a remote update source.              
  delta     Utilities for creating or applying delta packages.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Velopack CLI 0.0.1350-l10n.14-g48cbddf546 (prerelease), for distributing      
  applications.                                                                 

Usage:
  vpk [command] [options]  

Options:
  -h, -H, --help, --vhelp  Show help (-h) or extended help (-H).                
  -x, --legacyConsole      Disable console colors and interactive               
                           components.                                          
  -y, --yes                'yes' by instead of 'no' in non-interactive          
                           prompts.                                             
  --verbose                Print diagnostic messages.                           
  --skip-updates           Skip update checks                                   

Commands:
  pack      Create a Linux .AppImage bundle from application files.         
  download  Download's the latest release from a remote update source.      
  upload    Upload local package(s) to a remote update source.              
  delta     Utilities for creating or applying delta packages.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Utilities for creating or applying delta packages.  

Usage:
  vpk delta [command] [options]  

Commands:
  generate  Generate a delta patch from two full releases.                   
  patch     Patch a base package and retrieve the original new package.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Utilities for creating or applying delta packages.  

Usage:
  vpk delta [command] [options]  

Commands:
  generate  Generate a delta patch from two full releases.                   
  patch     Patch a base package and retrieve the original new package.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate a delta patch from two full releases.  

Usage:
  vpk delta generate [options]  

Options:
  --mode <BestSize|BestSpeed|None>  Set the delta generation mode. [            
                                    default: BestSpeed]                         
  -b, --base <PATH> (REQ)           The base package for the created            
                                    patch.                                      
  -n, --new <PATH> (REQ)            The resulting package for the created       
                                    patch.                                      
  -o, --output <PATH> (REQ)         The output file path for the created        
                                    patch.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Generate a delta patch from two full releases.  

Usage:
  vpk delta generate [options]  

Options:
  --mode <BestSize|BestSpeed|None>  Set the delta generation mode. [            
                                    default: BestSpeed]                         
  -b, --base <PATH> (REQ)           The base package for the created            
                                    patch.                                      
  -n, --new <PATH> (REQ)            The resulting package for the created       
                                    patch.                                      
  -o, --output <PATH> (REQ)         The output file path for the created        
                                    patch.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Patch a base package and retrieve the original new package.  

Usage:
  vpk delta patch [options]  

Options:
  -b, --base <PATH> (REQ)    The base package for the created patch.           
  -p, --patch <PATH>         The resulting package for the created patch.      
  -o, --output <PATH> (REQ)  The output file path for the created patch.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Patch a base package and retrieve the original new package.  

Usage:
  vpk delta patch [options]  

Options:
  -b, --base <PATH> (REQ)    The base package for the created patch.           
  -p, --patch <PATH>         The resulting package for the created patch.      
  -o, --output <PATH> (REQ)  The output file path for the created patch.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Download's the latest release from a remote update source.  

Usage:
  vpk download [command] [options]  

Commands:
  github  Download latest release from GitHub repository.                    
  gitea   Download latest release from Gitea repository.                     
  s3      Download latest release from an S3 bucket.                         
  az      Download latest release from an Azure Blob Storage container.      
  local   Download latest release from a local path or network share.        
  http    Download latest release from a HTTP source.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Download's the latest release from a remote update source.  

Usage:
  vpk download [command] [options]  

Commands:
  github  Download latest release from GitHub repository.                    
  gitea   Download latest release from Gitea repository.                     
  s3      Download latest release from an S3 bucket.                         
  az      Download latest release from an Azure Blob Storage container.      
  local   Download latest release from a local path or network share.        
  http    Download latest release from a HTTP source.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Download latest release from an Azure Blob Storage container.  

Usage:
  vpk download az [options]  

Options:
  -o, --outputDir <DIR>      Output directory for created packages. [           
                             default: Releases]                                 
  -c, --channel <NAME>       The channel to use for this release. [             
                             default: linux]                                    
  --account <ACCOUNT> (REQ)  Account name                                       
  --key <KEY>                Account secret key                                 
  --sas <TOKEN>              Shared access signature token (not the url)        
  --container <NAME> (REQ)   Azure container name                               
  --endpoint <URL>           Service url (eg.                                   
                             https://<account-name>.blob.core.windows.net)      
  --timeout <MINUTES>        Network timeout in minutes. [default: 30]

commands.payload:

  --timeout <MINUTES>        Network timeout in minutes. [default: 30]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Download latest release from Gitea repository.  

Usage:
  vpk download gitea [options]  

Options:
  -o, --outputDir <DIR>  Output directory for created packages. [default:       
                         Releases]                                              
  -c, --channel <NAME>   The channel to use for this release. [default:         
                         linux]                                                 
  --repoUrl (REQ)        Full url to the gitea repository (eg.                  
                         'https://gitea.com/myname/myrepo').                    
  --token                OAuth token to use as login credentials.               
  --timeout <MINUTES>    Network timeout in minutes. [default: 30]              
  --pre                  Get latest pre-release instead of stable.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Download latest release from an S3 bucket.  

Usage:
  vpk download s3 [options]  

Options:
  -o, --outputDir <DIR>      Output directory for created packages. [           
                             default: Releases]                                 
  -c, --channel <NAME>       The channel to use for this release. [             
                             default: linux]                                    
  --keyId <KEYID>            Authentication identifier or access key.           
  --secret <KEY>             Authentication secret key.                         
  --region <REGION>          AWS service region (eg. us-west-1).                
  --session <TOKEN>          Authentication session token.                      
  --endpoint <URL>           Custom S3-compatible service url (backblaze,       
                             digital ocean, etc).                               
  --bucket <NAME> (REQ)      Name of the S3 bucket.                             
  --prefix <PREFIX>          Prefix to the S3 url.                              
  --disablePathStyle <BOOL>  Disable the default of path-style endpoint         
                             and use a subdomain endpoint instead.              
  --timeout <MINUTES>        Network timeout in minutes. [default: 30]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create a Linux .AppImage bundle from application files.  

Usage:
  vpk pack [options]  

Options:
  -o, --outputDir <DIR>              Output directory for created               
                                     packages. [default: Releases]              
  -c, --channel <NAME>               The channel to use for this release.       
                                     [default: linux]                           
  -r, --runtime <RID>                The target runtime to build packages       
                                     for.                                       
  -u, --packId <ID> (REQ)            Unique Id for application bundle.          
  -v, --packVersion <VERSION> (REQ)  Current version for application            
                                     bundle.                                    
  -p, --packDir <DIR> (REQ)          Directory containing application           
                                     files for release.                         
  --localization <DIR>               Path to a folder containing localized      
                                     dialog files grouped by culture.           
                                     Supports PO and XLIFF formats (for         
                                     example 'en/velopack.po',                  
                                     'fr/velopack.xlf').                        
  --packAuthors <AUTHORS>            Company name or comma-delimited list       
                                     of authors.                                
  --packTitle <NAME>                 Display/friendly name for                  
                                     application.                               
  --releaseNotes <PATH>              File with markdown-formatted notes         
                                     for this version.                          
  --delta <BestSize|BestSpeed|None>  Disable or set the delta generation        
                                     mode. [default: BestSpeed]                 
  -i, --icon <PATH>                  Path to icon file for package.             
  -e, --mainExe <NAME>               The file name (not path) of the            
                                     main/entry executable.                     
  --exclude <REGEX>                  A regex which excludes matched files       
                                     from the package. [default: .*\.pdb]       
  --categories <NAMES>               Categories from the freedesktop.org        
                                     Desktop Menu spec                          
  --compression <ALGO>               Set the compression algorithm to use       
                                     for the AppImage [default: gzip]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Upload local package(s) to a remote update source.  

Usage:
  vpk upload [command] [options]  

Commands:
  github  Upload releases to a GitHub repository.                  
  gitea   Upload releases to a Gitea repository.                   
  s3      Upload releases to a S3 bucket.                          
  az      Upload releases to an Azure Blob Storage container.      
  local   Upload releases to a local path or network share.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Upload local package(s) to a remote update source.  

Usage:
  vpk upload [command] [options]  

Commands:
  github  Upload releases to a GitHub repository.                  
  gitea   Upload releases to a Gitea repository.                   
  s3      Upload releases to a S3 bucket.                          
  az      Upload releases to an Azure Blob Storage container.      
  local   Upload releases to a local path or network share.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Upload releases to an Azure Blob Storage container.  

Usage:
  vpk upload az [options]  

Options:
  -o, --outputDir <DIR> (REQ)  Output directory for created packages. [         
                               default: Releases]                               
  -c, --channel <NAME>         The channel to use for this release. [           
                               default: linux]                                  
  --account <ACCOUNT> (REQ)    Account name                                     
  --key <KEY>                  Account secret key                               
  --sas <TOKEN>                Shared access signature token (not the url)      
  --container <NAME> (REQ)     Azure container name                             
  --endpoint <URL>             Service url (eg.                                 
                               https://<account-name>.blob.core.windows.ne      
                               t)                                               
  --timeout <MINUTES>          Network timeout in minutes. [default: 30]        
  --keepMaxReleases <COUNT>    The maximum number of releases to keep in        
                               the container, anything older will be            
                               deleted.

commands.payload:
Description:

  --endpoint <URL>             Service url (eg.                                 
                               https://<account-name>.blob.core.windows.ne

result.stdout:
Description:
  Upload releases to a Gitea repository.  

Usage:
  vpk upload gitea [options]  

Options:
  -o, --outputDir <DIR> (REQ)  Output directory for created packages. [         
                               default: Releases]                               
  -c, --channel <NAME>         The channel to use for this release. [           
                               default: linux]                                  
  --repoUrl (REQ)              Full url to the gitea repository (eg.            
                               'https://gitea.com/myname/myrepo').              
  --token                      OAuth token to use as login credentials.         
  --timeout <MINUTES>          Network timeout in minutes. [default: 30]        
  --publish                    Create and publish instead of leaving as         
                               draft.                                           
  --pre                        Create as pre-release instead of stable.         
  --merge                      Allow merging this upload with an existing       
                               release.                                         
  --releaseName <NAME>         A custom name for the release.                   
  --tag <NAME>                 A custom tag for the release.                    
  --targetCommitish <NAME>     A commitish value for tag (branch or commit      
                               SHA).

result.stdout:
Description:
  Upload releases to a local path or network share.  

Usage:
  vpk upload local [options]  

Options:
  -o, --outputDir <DIR> (REQ)  Output directory for created packages. [         
                               default: Releases]                               
  -c, --channel <NAME>         The channel to use for this release. [           
                               default: linux]                                  
  --path <PATH> (REQ)          Target file path to copy releases to/from.       
  --keepMaxReleases <COUNT>    The maximum number of releases to keep in        
                               the target directory, anything older will        
                               be deleted.                                      
  --regenerate                 Force regenerate the                             
                               releases.{channel}.json file in the target       
                               directory.

Description:
  Upload releases to a S3 bucket.  

Usage:
  vpk upload s3 [options]  

Options:
  -o, --outputDir <DIR> (REQ)  Output directory for created packages. [         
                               default: Releases]                               
  -c, --channel <NAME>         The channel to use for this release. [           
                               default: linux]                                  
  --keyId <KEYID>              Authentication identifier or access key.         
  --secret <KEY>               Authentication secret key.                       
  --region <REGION>            AWS service region (eg. us-west-1).              
  --session <TOKEN>            Authentication session token.                    
  --endpoint <URL>             Custom S3-compatible service url                 
                               (backblaze, digital ocean, etc).                 
  --bucket <NAME> (REQ)        Name of the S3 bucket.                           
  --prefix <PREFIX>            Prefix to the S3 url.                            
  --disablePathStyle <BOOL>    Disable the default of path-style endpoint       
                               and use a subdomain endpoint instead.            
  --timeout <MINUTES>          Network timeout in minutes. [default: 30]        
  --keepMaxReleases <COUNT>    The maximum number of releases to keep in        
                               the bucket, anything older will be deleted.
